title: Media silent-failure audit: instrument top 10 of 24 swallowed-exception sites; surface video-gen chain exhaustion
current_state: tools/tools_media.py (1153 lines) contains 24 swallowed-exception sites across image gen, video gen, document read, and media analysis fallback chains. Known fragility: video generation's Replicate backend often 429s and the fallback chain (Replicate -> HuggingFace, T-121) can exhaust silently, returning {"success": false} with no explanation of what was tried. Same reusable infra as T-254: agent/observability.py track_silent() + data/silent_failures.db + silent_failure_watcher passive.
target_state: Top 10 sites by user impact instrumented with track_silent('media.<site>', e). Video-gen chain exhaustion specifically returns a user-visible message naming what happened ('backend busy, tried Replicate + HF') instead of a bare success:false. Fallback chains still fall back - visibility only, no control-flow change.
migration_plan: 1. Grep tools/tools_media.py for the 24 swallow sites; rank: generation-request failures > fallback-chain exhaustion > document-parse failures > internal cleanup.

3. In the video-gen chain-exhaustion path, build the error string from the backends actually attempted (e.g. 'video generation unavailable: Replicate 429, HuggingFace timeout') and return it in the tool result.

4. Test: testing/test_media_silent_failures.py - monkeypatch both video backends to raise, call generate_video, assert result contains the names of both attempted backends and a row lands in the silent-failures store.
risk_notes: Do not add retries or new fallback logic - that is the rejected offline-retry-queue scope. Visibility only. Costly-test discipline: the new test must use monkeypatched backends, never real Replicate/HF calls, so it stays in verify's non-costly set.
